practices.name: Artshack Brooklyn
practices.what: Nonprofit ceramics studio. Wheel throwing, handbuilding, sculpture; sessions include unlimited open studio.
practices.caveat: Prices are not published; email info@artshackbrooklyn.org. Free community days run year-round. Work-study is 50% off. Full scholarships for Black and Brown applicants; fall apps open Jul 28.

practices.name: Gasworks NYC
practices.what: Wheel throwing, handbuilding, cone 10 gas reduction firing.

practices.name: Bushwick Ceramics
practices.what: Wheel throwing and handbuilding; one-day workshops; open studio membership.

practices.name: BKLYN CLAY
practices.what: Semester-based wheel throwing and handbuilding, open studio included. Latin American ceramic traditions track in Spanish and English.
practices.caveat: $825 for the full 12-week semester, plus a $15 tool kit and firing fees at 4 cents per cubic inch.

practices.name: Choplet Ceramic Studio
practices.what: Monthly unlimited-access membership; wheel and handbuilding. Also runs screenprint boot camps.

practices.name: Brooklyn Clay Industries
practices.what: BYOB drop-in wheel and handbuilding.

practices.name: Powerhouse Arts Community Ceramics Studio
practices.what: Wheel, handbuilding, slip-casting, gas firing, raku, 3D printing. Shares a building with MGC printmaking.

practices.name: Manny Cantor Center
practices.what: Adult wheel and handbuilding at the Educational Alliance settlement house.
practices.caveat: Income-eligible scholarships available.

practices.name: Scratch Ceramics
practices.what: Community ceramics studio; wheel throwing and handbuilding. The Queens hub since 2024.

practices.name: Uptown Clay
practices.what: Community ceramics studio, 20 wheels and 4 kilns. Instructors are Washington Heights natives. Drop-in option.

schedule.note: Group sessions, drop-in a la carte, and membership. Open studio Sundays 11am-10pm

practices.name: MGC Community Print Studio
practices.what: Intaglio, relief, lithography, screenprinting, digital. The largest community print studio in Brooklyn.

schedule.note: Semester-based; summer/fall not posted as of Jul 6

practices.caveat: No posted semester since June 23. Email mgc@powerhousearts.org.

availability.note: No posted summer or fall semester since June 23.

practices.name: Shoestring Press
practices.what: Membership print shop five minutes from home. Screenprint, etching, litho, relief. Master printer Lane Sell.

schedule.note: Membership studio; classes at all levels, single-day and multi-session

practices.caveat: Visit by appointment. Email info@shoestringpressny.com. 640 Classon Ave, between Bergen and Dean.

practices.name: Robert Blackburn Printmaking Workshop
practices.what: Fine art printmaking: screenprinting, photopolymer letterpress, textile printing. Eight hours of free studio time after each workshop.

practices.name: Lower East Side Printshop
practices.what: Screenprinting, intaglio, drypoint, chine collé. One-day workshops with studio time included.

schedule.note: One-day workshops; open membership for ongoing access

practices.name: Bushwick Print Lab
practices.what: Screenprinting workshops. One beginner workshop unlocks open studio rental.

schedule.note: Workshops on rotation; open studio after beginner class

practices.name: Works in Progress NYC
practices.what: Commercial-grade silk screen intensive: coating, burning, washing screens.

schedule.note: Intro intensives; open studio for members

practices.name: Mobile Print Power
practices.what: Multigenerational silkscreen and political education collective. Portable print carts, citywide pop-ups.
practices.caveat: Email info@mobileprintpower.com to join the Tuesday workshop. All welcome.

practices.name: Secret Riso Club
practices.what: Community bookstore and risograph studio. Riso 101 gets you a two-color poster and ten copies to take home.

schedule.note: Riso 101 monthly; workshops on rotation; member studio access after certification

practices.caveat: Community/scholarship tickets on request: workshops@secretrisoclub.com.

practices.name: Lucky Risograph
practices.what: Community riso press. Three-hour group workshops; you leave with printed work.

practices.name: Center for Book Arts
practices.what: Letterpress, bookbinding, paper marbling, typography, artist books. Over 100 classes a year.
practices.caveat: Pay-what-you-can intensives offered periodically. $75/yr membership gives class discounts.

practices.name: Textile Arts Center
practices.what: Weaving, knitting, sewing, natural dyeing, felting, visible mending. The widest range of fiber process in Brooklyn.
practices.caveat: No sliding scale listed. TAC AIR residency exists for fiber artists.

practices.name: UrbanGlass
practices.what: Glassblowing, flameworking, stained glass, coldworking, fused glass, beadmaking.
practices.caveat: Prices are not listed per class; call (718) 625-3685 or email info@urbanglass.org.

practices.name: Brooklyn Glass
practices.what: Glassblowing, flameworking, neon fabrication. Scientific glassblowing track.

practices.name: Makeville Studio
practices.what: Community woodshop. Furniture making, wood turning, hand tools. Certification unlocks hourly bench rental.

schedule.note: Classes required before certification, then hourly bench rental

practices.caveat: Prices are not published; contact hello@makeville.com.

practices.name: Craftsman Ave
practices.what: Stained glass, woodworking, blacksmithing, welding, leatherwork, knife-making, electronics. Four-hour beginner workshops, materials included.

practices.name: MakerSpace NYC
practices.what: 50,000 sq ft nonprofit. Woodshop, metalshop, welding, blacksmithing, ceramics, CNC, 3D printing.
practices.caveat: Free 3-month memberships by competitive application; priority to Sunset Park residents and underrepresented artists.

practices.name: Brooklyn Spark
practices.what: Nine-founder co-op maker space. Woodshop, ceramics with kiln, textiles and looms, laser cutter, 3D printer.
practices.caveat: Monday public nights 7-10pm are free and need no membership. That is the way in.

practices.name: Bushwick Community Darkroom
practices.what: Film developing, B&W and color printing. Hourly and monthly rental; 24/7 member access.
practices.caveat: Membership starts at $30/month. Non-member rental starts at $20/hr. Most group classes are $195 for a 3-hour session. Public hours Tue-Sat 12-8pm.

practices.name: Gowanus Darkroom
practices.what: Small-group workshops, max four: film developing, B&W and color printing, photograms. Monthly group critique.

schedule.note: $12/hr rental; workshops on rotation; monthly critique

practices.caveat: Monthly critique is $5 to show, free to attend as a listener, free for members. Cheapest accountability structure on this list.

practices.name: Penumbra Foundation
practices.what: Tintype, wet plate collodion, cyanotype, gum bichromate, DIY developers. Over 100 workshops a year.
practices.caveat: Artist lecture series is free and open to the public.

practices.name: International Center of Photography
practices.what: Darkroom skills, film developing, alt processes, B&W printing.
practices.caveat: Member discounts 10-40% off tuition.

practices.name: AlterWork Studios
practices.what: Multi-disciplinary open access: ceramics, woodworking, printmaking, darkroom, screen printing, textiles. Open daily.

schedule.note: 6-week class cycles year-round. Studio open daily 12-9pm

practices.caveat: Free class via 3 hr/week studio assistant work exchange: tina@alterworkstudios.com. Address is 40-20 22nd St; CourseHorse's 30-09 35th Ave listing is wrong.

practices.name: Mono No Aware
practices.what: 16mm Bolex, Super 8, optical printing, hand-processed reversal. Stock, processing, and gear included. Workshops end in a free public screening.

schedule.note: Workshops on rotation. Closed all of August; September is the beginner slate

practices.caveat: Every workshop ends in a free public screening. Stock, processing, cameras, and editing are included.

availability.note: Closed July 3 and all of August. September is the beginner slate.

practices.name: Millennium Film Workshop
practices.what: Optical printing, 16mm, Super 8, Steenbeck editing. One of the only places in NYC still teaching Super 8.

schedule.note: $80/yr 8mm tier includes workshops + Super 8 cameras + editing; $150/yr 16mm tier includes everything

practices.caveat: No standalone class registration; workshops come with membership. The /workshops-and-classes URL 404s. Visiting hours Mon-Wed + Fri 11am-4pm.

practices.name: 343 Labs
practices.what: Ableton Live production: intro workshops, 4-week intensives, full courses.

practices.name: Brooklyn Conservatory of Music
practices.what: Jazz Lab intensive for adults: small-group ensemble, improvisation, performance development.
practices.caveat: Full and partial scholarships available.

practices.name: Creative Music Studio at Ibeam
practices.what: Improvisation workshops in free jazz, electroacoustic, experimental sound. Backline provided. All backgrounds welcome.

practices.name: Pioneer Works
practices.what: Multi-disciplinary nonprofit: recording studio, creative coding, ceramics, Synth Library. Free monthly open house on second Sundays.
practices.caveat: 85% of programs are free or low-cost. Second Sundays is free with RSVP. Synth Library gives free instrument access; confirm hours.

practices.name: School for Poetic Computation
practices.what: Code, hardware, critical theory, and art. One-day public workshops and free public talks.

schedule.note: One-day workshops plus free public talks

practices.caveat: Workshops are $300 flat plus fees. Public talks are free. Scholarship tickets released to the email list two weeks ahead.

practices.name: NYC Resistor
practices.what: Hacker collective and shared shop. Soldering, electronics, PCB design, wax casting, 3D modeling.
practices.caveat: Public Craft Nights Mon + Thu are free with RSVP. 18+. Need-based tickets on some paid classes.

practices.name: Interference Archive
practices.what: Volunteer-run social movement archive with open stacks. Zine workshops, collections work parties, fiber meetups.

schedule.note: Workshops, work parties, and zine club on rotation

practices.name: Booklyn
practices.what: Zines 101 for adults: history, binding, printing, distribution. Zines as community practice.

schedule.note: Workshops at Old Stone House and Interference Archive

practices.name: NY Writers Coalition
practices.what: Free drop-in creative writing workshops. Write to prompts, share if you want, positive and specific feedback only. No critique.
practices.caveat: Runs at 40+ sites citywide. The Eastern Parkway branch is walking distance; the Grand Army Plaza session is Mondays 3-5pm, bilingual.

practices.name: Brooklyn Poets
practices.what: Free weekly community writing groups: prompts and optional share-out.
practices.caveat: Community groups are free. Paid multi-week workshops also run.

practices.name: Center for Fiction
practices.what: Fiction and nonfiction workshops, open enrollment.
practices.caveat: The site blocks automated readers; check prices in a browser.

practices.name: Belladonna* Writing Spirals
practices.what: Feminist avant-garde literary press. Each session works from a chaplet in their archive.
practices.caveat: 925 Bergen St #405, walking distance. NOTAFLOF: no one turned away for lack of funds.

practices.name: Gotham Writers Workshop
practices.what: Fiction, poetry, nonfiction, screenwriting. The largest creative writing school in the city.
practices.caveat: The site blocks automated readers; check prices in a browser.

practices.name: Wondrous
practices.what: The only NYC studio dedicated entirely to breaking. Adult beginner class is toprock and footwork, no strength prerequisites. History and culture taught alongside technique.
practices.caveat: No regular class schedule and the website is down; whether classes are running at all is unknown. Check @wondrousnyc.

availability.note: Unlocked as an anchor: no regular class schedule, and the website is down. Whether they are actively holding classes at all is unknown.

practices.name: Ailey Extension
practices.what: Vogue, heels, Afro'Dance, West African, house. Dense weeknight schedule.
practices.caveat: Dense weeknight schedule in Midtown. Book through Mindbody at ailey.org/classes.

practices.name: Mover's Bodega
practices.what: House, waacking, breaking, hip hop, lite feet, popping, jersey club.
practices.caveat: $20/class; 3-pack $55; 6-pack $108.

practices.name: Capoeira Luanda Brooklyn
practices.what: Capoeira Angola with Professor Pião, Mestre Jelon lineage from Bahia. Movement, music, song, and ritual are inseparable in the form.
practices.caveat: CORRECTED Jul 14: the site lists Mon/Wed/Fri 7:40-9:00pm at Dean St. Playground, not 6:30-8pm at the Co-op School. Org address is 644 Gates Ave; classes are at the playground. Prices are not published and the previously-noted free trial is unconfirmed. Call (917) 224-1209.

practices.name: The Humanist Project
practices.what: Eight-week weekly clown training with Michael F. Toomey, Gaulier and Fusetti lineage. Poke at the ridiculous; every slip, fall, and heartbreak.
practices.caveat: 2026 fall dates STILL not posted; site shows the Sep 15-Nov 3 2025 cycle. Working estimate Sep 14-Nov 2, 2026. Tiered tuition and payment plans on request. Email thehumanistproject.org/contact.

availability.note: 2026 fall dates still not posted; the site shows the Sep 15 - Nov 3, 2025 cycle.

practices.name: JACK
practices.what: Performance and civic space, 80+ shows a year. Rehearsal rental subsidized by NYSCA for artistic projects.

schedule.note: Rehearsal rental by booking; programming year-round

practices.caveat: One C-train stop from Crown Heights. Rehearsal rental is NYSCA-subsidized and applies to artistic projects; confirm the rate at rentals@jackny.org.

practices.name: The Bushwick Starr
practices.what: Free community workshops in August: Creating Performance (devised work), bilingual storytelling.
practices.caveat: Summers at the Starr runs free community workshops in August, including devised-performance and bilingual storytelling. Specific dates not yet posted.

availability.note: Summers at the Starr runs in August; specific dates not yet posted.

practices.name: Theatre of the Oppressed NYC
practices.what: Boal's Forum Theatre methodology: a play about an unresolved oppression, then the audience steps in and improvises alternatives. Concrete facilitation tools.

schedule.note: Level 1 training, roughly twice a year: 4 sessions online/in-person plus 2 in person

practices.caveat: Sliding scale by financial means; no institutional affiliation required. Shorter intro workshops run $35-80. The most directly relevant facilitation practice in the city for participatory work.

availability.note: Level 1 training runs roughly twice a year. Waitlist for the fall 2026 cohort.

practices.name: The Fire Ensemble
practices.what: Intergenerational pop-up choir. Gospel, R&B, musical theater. Music and ritual framed as tools for collective liberation.
practices.caveat: Spring session ended in June; summer is the gap. Fall session TBD, likely September. RSVP now at fireensemble.com/choir-form. Correct site is fireensemble.com, NOT singforhope.org.

availability.note: Spring 2026 session ended in June. The ensemble runs spring and fall sessions; summer is the gap.

practices.name: NYC Sacred Harp
practices.what: Leaderless four-part communal singing in a hollow square. Anyone can lead, no soloists, no audience, no performance. Loaner books; you do not need to read music.
practices.caveat: St. Paul's Episcopal, 199 Carroll St. Free, pass the hat. A 250-year-old participatory form with consent structure built into the democracy of it.

practices.name: Brooklynite Choir
practices.what: Pop, rock, indie. No audition, no sheet music, learn by ear.
practices.caveat: Currently full; on the waitlist. Fall cycle likely opens August.

availability.note: Currently full. Fall cycle likely opens in August.

practices.name: Flux Factory
practices.what: Artist-led nonprofit space. Open studios, salons, workshops. Shared music studio, woodshop, screen printing.

practices.name: Art Students League
practices.what: Open enrollment, no application or jury: painting, sculpture, drawing, printmaking. Since 1875.

schedule.note: Short-term memberships and drop-in open sessions

practices.name: Materials for the Arts
practices.what: Creative reuse studio workshops. Free supply warehouse for qualifying NYC nonprofits.

schedule.note: Workshops, mostly for educators; some public field trips

practices.caveat: Primary audience is educators with CTLE credits. Public access is limited.

practices.name: Abrons Arts Center
practices.what: Multi-disciplinary community arts inside Henry Street Settlement since 1975. Ceramics, visual arts, digital music, theater, dance.
practices.caveat: "Free and affordable" is a stated commitment; free for NYCHA residents. Summer is light on visual arts; check directly.

practices.name: Taller Boricua
practices.what: Puerto Rican printmaking collective founded 1969. Litho, silkscreen, linocut, intaglio, wood engraving.

schedule.note: Access is through the collective, not open enrollment

practices.caveat: No public enrollment schedule. Contact info@tallerboricua.org. Gallery at 1680 Lexington is open Tue-Sat.

availability.note: No public enrollment schedule; access is through the collective.

practices.name: Bronx River Art Center
practices.what: Wheel throwing, handbuilding, printmaking, stained glass. Materials included.
practices.caveat: Fee waivers and scholarships available. Roughly 60-75 minutes from Crown Heights.

practices.name: Art Lab at Snug Harbor
practices.what: Ceramics, printmaking, stained glass, darkroom, sewing, jewelry, woodworking. Nonprofit since 1975.
practices.caveat: 90 minutes from Crown Heights. Listed for completeness, not as a realistic option.
